rawText:
President Pitzer, Mr. Vice President, Governor, Congressman Thomas, Senator Wiley, and Congressman Miller, Mr. Webb. Mr. Bell, scientists, distinguished guests, and ladies and gentlemen:

I appreciate your president having made me an honorary visiting professor, and I will assure you that my first lecture will be very brief.

I am delighted to be here and I'm particularly delighted to be here on this occasion.

We meet at a college noted for knowledge, in a city noted for progress, in a State noted for strength, and we stand in need of all three, for we meet in an hour of change and challenge, in a decade of hope and fear, in an age of both knowledge and ignorance. The greater our knowledge increases, the greater our ignorance unfolds.

Despite the striking fact that most of the scientists that the world has ever known are alive and working today, despite the fact that this Nation's own scientific manpower is doubling every 12 years in a rate of growth more than three times that of our population as a whole, despite that, the vast stretches of the unknown and the unanswered and the unfinished still far outstrip our collective comprehension.

No man can fully grasp how far and how fast we have come, but condense, if you will, the 50,000 years of man's recorded history in a time span of but a half-century. Stated in these terms, we know very little about the first 40 years, except at the end of them advanced man had learned to use the skins of animals to cover them. Then about 10 years ago, under this standard, man emerged from his caves to construct other kinds of shelter. Only 5 years ago man learned to write and use a cart with wheels. Christianity began less than 2 years ago. The printing press came this year, and then less than 2 months ago, during this whole 50-year span of human history, the steam engine provided a new source of power.

Newton explored the meaning of gravity. Last month electric lights and telephones and automobiles and airplanes became available. Only last week did we develop penicillin and television and nuclear power, and now if America's new spacecraft succeeds in reaching Venus, we will have literally reached the stars before midnight tonight.

This is a breathtaking pace, and such a pace cannot help but create new ills as it dispels old, new ignorance, new problems, new dangers. Surely the opening vistas of space promise high costs and hardships, as well as high reward.

So it is not surprising that some would have us stay where we are a little longer to rest, to wait. But this city of Houston, this State of Texas, this country of the United States was not built by those who waited and rested and wished to look behind them. This country was conquered by those who moved forward--and so will space.

William Bradford, speaking in 1630 of the founding of the Plymouth Bay Colony, said that all great and honorable actions are accompanied with great difficulties, and both must be enterprised and overcome with answerable courage.

If this capsule history of our progress teaches us anything, it is that man, in his quest for knowledge and progress, is determined and cannot be deterred. The exploration of space will go ahead, whether we join in it or not, and it is one of the great adventures of all time, and no nation which expects to be the leader of other nations can expect to stay behind in this race for space.

overview.title: We Choose to Go to the Moon
overview.source: Address at Rice University — John F. Kennedy, September 12, 1962

sentences.original: President Pitzer, Mr. Vice President, Governor, Congressman Thomas, Senator Wiley, and Congressman Miller, Mr. Webb, Mr. Bell, scientists, distinguished guests, and ladies and gentlemen:
sentences.translation: 피처 총장님, 부통령님, 주지사님, 토마스 하원의원님, 와일리 상원의원님, 밀러 하원의원님, 웹 국장님, 벨 박사님, 과학자 여러분, 귀빈 여러분, 그리고 신사 숙녀 여러분:

grammarElements.text: Mr. Vice President, Governor, Congressman Thomas, Senator Wiley, and Congressman Miller
grammarElements.label: 호격 (열거)

grammarElements.text: scientists, distinguished guests, and ladies and gentlemen
grammarElements.label: 호격 (일반 청중)

vocabulary.example: She is a distinguished professor of physics.
The congressman introduced a new bill.
Senator Wiley addressed the crowd.

expressions.usage: 공식 연설에서 청중을 호칭할 때 사용하는 격식 표현
공식 행사에서 초대된 중요 인사를 지칭할 때

patterns.pattern: [Title] [Name], [Title] [Name], ... and ladies and gentlemen:
patterns.explanation: 공식 연설의 전통적 오프닝. 참석 인사를 직위 순으로 호명한 뒤 일반 청중을 마지막에 호칭.
examples: Mr. President, members of Congress, and fellow citizens:
Mr. Chairman, members of the board, and esteemed colleagues:

drillQuestions.question: President Pitzer, Mr. Vice President, Governor, and _____ and gentlemen:
Scientists, _____ guests, and ladies and gentlemen:

sentences.notes: 미국 공식 연설의 전형적 오프닝. 직위가 높은 사람부터 낮은 순서로 호명하는 것이 프로토콜. President Pitzer는 라이스 대학교 총장, Mr. Webb는 NASA 국장.

koreanAlignment.en: President Pitzer
Mr. Vice President
Governor
Congressman Thomas, Senator Wiley, and Congressman Miller
koreanAlignment.ko: 토마스 하원의원님, 와일리 상원의원님, 밀러 하원의원님

koreanAlignment.en: scientists, distinguished guests
and ladies and gentlemen

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 호명법(roll call) — 참석 인사를 순서대로 호명하여 존경을 표하고 행사의 격을 높임

sentences.original: I appreciate your president having made me an honorary visiting professor, and I will assure you that my first lecture will be very brief.
sentences.translation: 저를 명예 방문 교수로 임명해주신 총장님께 감사드리며, 제 첫 강의는 매우 짧을 것이라고 장담합니다.

grammarElements.text: your president having made me an honorary visiting professor
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동명사 복합구조 — 소유격 + 동명사)

grammarElements.text: that my first lecture will be very brief
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: I appreciate your help with the project.
She received an honorary degree from the university.
I can assure you that everything is under control.

expressions.usage: 소유격 + 동명사 구문으로 격식 있는 감사 표현
자신의 약속이나 보장을 공식적으로 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I appreciate [someone] having [past participle] ..., and I will assure you that ...
patterns.explanation: 감사 인사 뒤에 유머러스한 약속을 연결하는 연설 오프닝 패턴.
examples: I appreciate the committee having invited me, and I will assure you that my remarks will be brief.
I appreciate your having taken the time, and I will assure you it will be worthwhile.

drillQuestions.question: I appreciate your president having _____ me an honorary professor.
I will _____ you that my lecture will be brief.

sentences.notes: "your president having made me"에서 소유격(your president) + 동명사(having made)는 격식체에서 사용하는 고급 문법. 구어에서는 "your president making me"가 더 흔하다. 이 문장은 연설의 유머 포인트로, "강의"가 곧 긴 연설이 될 것임을 암시하는 아이러니.

koreanAlignment.en: I appreciate
your president having made me
an honorary visiting professor
and I will assure you
that my first lecture will be very brief
koreanAlignment.ko: 제 첫 강의는 매우 짧을 것이라고

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 아이러니(irony) — "짧은 강의"라 했지만 실제로는 긴 역사적 연설이 될 것임을 암시하며 웃음 유도

sentences.original: I am delighted to be here and I'm particularly delighted to be here on this occasion.
sentences.translation: 이 자리에 함께하게 되어 기쁘고, 특히 이 행사에 함께할 수 있어 매우 기쁩니다.

grammarElements.text: to be here on this occasion
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to부정사 + 전치사구)

vocabulary.example: We are delighted to announce the winner.
I am particularly interested in this topic.
This is a special occasion for all of us.

expressions.phrase: I am delighted to be here
expressions.usage: 공식 연설에서 감사와 기쁨을 표현하는 격식 인사

patterns.pattern: I am [adjective] to [verb] ... and particularly [adjective] to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 감정을 표현한 뒤 particularly로 그 감정을 강화하는 반복 패턴.
examples: I am honored to receive this award and particularly honored to receive it from you.
I am grateful to be here and particularly grateful for the warm welcome.

drillQuestions.question: I am _____ to be here on this occasion.
I'm _____ delighted to be here on this occasion.

sentences.notes: 같은 구조(delighted to be here)를 반복하면서 particularly로 강화하는 수사법. 연설의 워밍업 단계로, 청중과의 유대감을 형성.

koreanAlignment.en: I am delighted to be here
and I'm particularly delighted
to be here on this occasion

sentences.original: We meet at a college noted for knowledge, in a city noted for progress, in a State noted for strength, and we stand in need of all three, for we meet in an hour of change and challenge, in a decade of hope and fear, in an age of both knowledge and ignorance.
sentences.translation: 우리는 지식으로 유명한 대학에서, 발전으로 유명한 도시에서, 힘으로 유명한 주에서 만나고 있으며, 우리에게는 이 세 가지 모두가 필요합니다. 변화와 도전의 시대에, 희망과 두려움의 10년에, 지식과 무지가 공존하는 시대에 우리는 만나고 있기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: at a college noted for knowledge
grammarElements.label: 장소 전치사구 (1 — 대학)

grammarElements.text: in a city noted for progress
grammarElements.label: 장소 전치사구 (2 — 도시)

grammarElements.text: in a State noted for strength
grammarElements.label: 장소 전치사구 (3 — 주)

grammarElements.text: and we stand in need of all three
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: for we meet in an hour of change and challenge, in a decade of hope and fear, in an age of both knowledge and ignorance
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (for — 3중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The region is noted for its wine production.
The country stands in need of reform.
Ignorance of the law is no excuse.

expressions.usage: known for의 격식 표현. 기관이나 장소의 특성을 설명할 때

expressions.phrase: an hour of change and challenge

patterns.pattern: at [place A] noted for [X], in [place B] noted for [Y], in [place C] noted for [Z]
patterns.explanation: 3중 병렬 구조(tricolon). 장소의 규모를 대학→도시→주로 확대하며 각각의 특성을 열거.
examples: at a school known for discipline, in a town known for unity, in a country known for resilience
at a lab noted for precision, in a company noted for innovation, in an industry noted for disruption

drillQuestions.question: We meet at a college _____ for knowledge.
We stand in _____ of all three.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 케네디 연설의 수사적 핵심. 3중 병렬 구조(tricolon)가 두 번 사용된다: (1) college/city/State + noted for, (2) hour/decade/age + of. 규모가 점점 커지는 확대법(amplification)으로, 로컬(대학)에서 시작하여 시대적(age) 규모로 확장한다. for는 because의 격식 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: We meet at a college noted for knowledge
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 지식으로 유명한 대학에서 만나고 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: in a city noted for progress
in a State noted for strength
and we stand in need of all three
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 우리에게는 이 세 가지 모두가 필요합니다

koreanAlignment.en: for we meet in an hour of change and challenge
koreanAlignment.ko: 변화와 도전의 시대에 만나고 있기 때문입니다

koreanAlignment.en: in a decade of hope and fear
in an age of both knowledge and ignorance

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 3중 병렬(tricolon) + 확대법(amplification) — 대학→도시→주, 시간→10년→시대로 규모를 확장하며 웅장함을 구축

sentences.original: The greater our knowledge increases, the greater our ignorance unfolds.
sentences.translation: 우리의 지식이 늘어날수록, 우리의 무지도 더 크게 드러납니다.

vocabulary.example: The mystery slowly unfolded before our eyes.
The population continues to increase.

expressions.usage: "the + 비교급, the + 비교급" 구문으로 비례 관계를 표현

patterns.pattern: The [comparative] [subject] [verb], the [comparative] [subject] [verb].
patterns.explanation: "the + 비교급, the + 비교급" 구문. 두 현상이 비례적으로 변화함을 나타냄.
examples: The harder you work, the luckier you get.
The more you learn, the more you realize you don't know.

drillQuestions.question: _____ greater our knowledge increases, the greater our ignorance unfolds.
The greater our knowledge increases, the greater our ignorance _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 소크라테스의 "나는 내가 모른다는 것을 안다"와 같은 맥락의 역설(paradox). "the + 비교급, the + 비교급" 구문은 한국어의 "~할수록 ~하다"에 해당하며 격식체에서 자주 사용된다.

koreanAlignment.en: The greater our knowledge increases
the greater our ignorance unfolds

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설(paradox) — 지식의 증가가 무지의 확대를 가져온다는 철학적 역설

sentences.original: Despite the striking fact that most of the scientists that the world has ever known are alive and working today, despite the fact that this Nation's own scientific manpower is doubling every 12 years in a rate of growth more than three times that of our population as a whole, despite that, the vast stretches of the unknown and the unanswered and the unfinished still far outstrip our collective comprehension.
sentences.translation: 세상이 알아온 과학자 대부분이 오늘날 살아서 일하고 있다는 놀라운 사실에도 불구하고, 이 나라의 과학 인력이 전체 인구 증가율의 세 배 이상의 속도로 12년마다 두 배가 되고 있다는 사실에도 불구하고, 그럼에도 불구하고, 미지의 것과 답 없는 것과 미완의 것의 광대한 영역은 여전히 우리의 총체적 이해를 훨씬 넘어서고 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: Despite the striking fact that most of the scientists that the world has ever known are alive and working today
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 (despite — 1번째)

grammarElements.text: despite the fact that this Nation's own scientific manpower is doubling every 12 years in a rate of growth more than three times that of our population as a whole
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 (despite — 2번째)

grammarElements.text: the vast stretches of the unknown and the unanswered and the unfinished
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: There is a striking similarity between the two cases.
The project requires significant manpower.
Demand has outstripped supply.
The scale of the disaster was beyond comprehension.

expressions.usage: 양보를 나타내는 격식 표현. despite + 명사절

patterns.pattern: Despite [A], despite [B], despite that, [main clause]
patterns.explanation: 3중 despite 구조. 양보절을 세 번 반복하여 주절의 내용을 극적으로 강조.
examples: Despite the warnings, despite the evidence, despite that, people continued to ignore the danger.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the striking fact that most scientists are alive today, we still have much to learn.
The unknown still far _____ our collective comprehension.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 케네디 연설에서 가장 복잡한 문장 중 하나. despite가 세 번 반복되는 삼단 양보(triple concession) 구조로, 마지막 "despite that"은 앞의 두 절을 요약한다. the unknown/the unanswered/the unfinished는 형용사의 명사화(the + 형용사 = 추상명사).

koreanAlignment.en: Despite the striking fact that most of the scientists ... are alive and working today
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학자 대부분이 오늘날 살아서 일하고 있다는 놀라운 사실에도 불구하고

koreanAlignment.en: despite the fact that this Nation's own scientific manpower is doubling every 12 years
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 나라의 과학 인력이 12년마다 두 배가 되고 있다는 사실에도 불구하고

koreanAlignment.en: despite that
the vast stretches of the unknown and the unanswered and the unfinished
koreanAlignment.ko: 미지의 것과 답 없는 것과 미완의 것의 광대한 영역은

koreanAlignment.en: still far outstrip our collective comprehension
koreanAlignment.ko: 여전히 우리의 총체적 이해를 훨씬 넘어서고 있습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼단 양보(triple concession) + 점층법(climax) — despite를 세 번 쌓아 올려 주절의 "여전히 무지하다"는 메시지를 극대화

sentences.original: No man can fully grasp how far and how fast we have come, but condense, if you will, the 50,000 years of man's recorded history in a time span of but a half-century.
sentences.translation: 우리가 얼마나 멀리, 얼마나 빠르게 왔는지 완전히 이해할 수 있는 사람은 없습니다. 그러나 인류의 기록된 5만 년 역사를 반세기라는 시간 안에 압축해 보십시오.

grammarElements.text: how far and how fast we have come
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: the 50,000 years of man's recorded history
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: in a time span of but a half-century
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (시간)

vocabulary.example: It is difficult to grasp the scale of the universe.
Can you condense this report into two pages?
The project was completed in a short time span.

expressions.usage: 비유나 특이한 표현 앞뒤에 삽입하여 청중의 양해를 구할 때

patterns.pattern: No man can fully [verb] ..., but [imperative], if you will, ...
patterns.explanation: 인간의 한계를 인정한 뒤, 청중에게 사고 실험을 제안하는 패턴.
examples: No one can fully imagine the scale, but picture, if you will, a world without electricity.
No person can truly understand, but consider, if you will, the distance to the nearest star.

drillQuestions.question: No man can fully _____ how far we have come.
Condense, if you _____, the 50,000 years of history.

sentences.notes: "but"이 두 번 다른 뜻으로 사용된다: 첫 번째 but은 접속사(그러나), 두 번째 but은 부사(단지, only). "of but a half-century"에서 but = only. "if you will"은 삽입구로 "말하자면"이라는 양해의 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: No man can fully grasp
koreanAlignment.ko: 완전히 이해할 수 있는 사람은 없습니다

koreanAlignment.en: how far and how fast we have come
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 얼마나 멀리, 얼마나 빠르게 왔는지

koreanAlignment.en: but condense, if you will
the 50,000 years of man's recorded history
in a time span of but a half-century

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 사고 실험(thought experiment) — 청중에게 역사를 압축하여 상상하도록 유도하는 참여형 수사법

sentences.original: Stated in these terms, we know very little about the first 40 years, except at the end of them advanced man had learned to use the skins of animals to cover them.
sentences.translation: 이런 기준으로 말하면, 우리는 처음 40년에 대해 거의 아는 것이 없습니다. 다만 그 끝 무렵에 진보한 인간이 동물 가죽을 이용하여 몸을 가리는 법을 배웠다는 것 정도입니다.

grammarElements.text: very little about the first 40 years
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: except at the end of them advanced man had learned to use the skins of animals to cover them
grammarElements.label: 예외절 (except)

vocabulary.example: Simply stated, the plan is to expand overseas.
Advanced civilizations developed writing systems.

expressions.usage: 앞서 설정한 비유적 틀 안에서 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: Stated in these terms, we know very little about ..., except ...
patterns.explanation: 분사구문으로 전제를 설정하고, 제한된 지식을 예외절로 보충하는 패턴.
examples: Stated simply, we understand very little about the ocean, except that it covers most of the Earth.

drillQuestions.question: _____ in these terms, we know very little about the first 40 years.
Advanced man had learned to use the skins of animals to _____ them.

sentences.notes: "Stated in these terms"는 분사구문으로, "이런 기준(50년 비유)으로 표현하면"이라는 뜻. 여기서부터 케네디는 인류 5만 년 역사를 50년으로 압축한 비유를 전개한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Stated in these terms
we know very little about the first 40 years
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 처음 40년에 대해 거의 아는 것이 없습니다

koreanAlignment.en: except at the end of them
advanced man had learned to use the skins of animals to cover them
koreanAlignment.ko: 진보한 인간이 동물 가죽으로 몸을 가리는 법을 배웠다는 것

sentences.original: Then about 10 years ago, under this standard, man emerged from his caves to construct other kinds of shelter.
sentences.translation: 그런 다음, 이 기준으로 약 10년 전에 인간은 동굴에서 나와 다른 종류의 거처를 짓기 시작했습니다.

grammarElements.text: to construct other kinds of shelter
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: The sun emerged from behind the clouds.
They constructed a bridge over the river.
They found shelter from the storm.

expressions.usage: 앞서 설정한 비유적 기준을 상기시킬 때

patterns.pattern: Then about [time] ago, under this standard, [subject] [verb] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 시간 비유 내에서 사건을 배치하는 내러티브 패턴.
examples: Then about 5 years ago, under this framework, humans learned to farm to feed larger groups.

drillQuestions.question: Man _____ from his caves to construct shelter.
Under this _____, man emerged about 10 years ago.

sentences.notes: "under this standard"는 50년 비유 기준을 상기시키는 장치. 실제로는 약 1만 년 전 신석기 시대에 해당. man이 his로 받아지는 것은 당시의 언어 관습(현재는 gender-neutral 표현 권장).

koreanAlignment.en: Then about 10 years ago
under this standard
man emerged from his caves
to construct other kinds of shelter

sentences.original: Only 5 years ago man learned to write and use a cart with wheels.
sentences.translation: 불과 5년 전에야 인간은 문자를 쓰고 바퀴 달린 수레를 사용하는 법을 배웠습니다.

vocabulary.example: The farmer loaded the cart with hay.

patterns.pattern: Only [time] ago, [subject] learned to [verb A] and [verb B].
patterns.explanation: "Only"로 시작하여 문명 발전의 최근성을 강조하는 내러티브 패턴.
examples: Only a century ago, humans learned to fly and transmit radio signals.
Only decades ago, we learned to split the atom and orbit the Earth.

drillQuestions.question: _____ 5 years ago man learned to write.
Man learned to write and use a cart _____ wheels.

sentences.notes: 실제로는 약 5,000년 전(메소포타미아 쐐기문자, 바퀴의 발명)에 해당. 50년 비유에서 5년 전으로 표현함으로써 인류 문명의 놀라운 최근성을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: Only 5 years ago
man learned to write
and use a cart with wheels
koreanAlignment.ko: 바퀴 달린 수레를 사용하는 법을 배웠습니다

sentences.original: Christianity began less than 2 years ago.
sentences.translation: 기독교는 2년도 채 안 되어 시작되었습니다.

vocabulary.example: Christianity spread across Europe in the first millennium.

patterns.pattern: [Major event] began less than [time] ago.
patterns.explanation: 압축된 시간 척도에서 역사적 사건의 최근성을 극적으로 보여주는 패턴.
examples: Democracy began less than a year ago in this scale.
Modern medicine began just weeks ago.

drillQuestions.question: Christianity began _____ than 2 years ago in this scale.

sentences.notes: 실제로는 약 2,000년 전에 해당. 50년 비유에서 2년이 채 안 되는 것으로 표현. 짧은 문장으로 시간 압축의 극적 효과를 높인다.

koreanAlignment.en: Christianity began
less than 2 years ago

sentences.original: The printing press came this year, and then less than 2 months ago, during this whole 50-year span of human history, the steam engine provided a new source of power.
sentences.translation: 인쇄기는 올해 등장했고, 이 50년이라는 인류 역사 전체에서 불과 2개월도 안 되어 증기기관이 새로운 동력원을 제공했습니다.

grammarElements.text: during this whole 50-year span of human history
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부사구 (비유 상기)

vocabulary.example: The printing press revolutionized the spread of information.
The steam engine powered the Industrial Revolution.
Wind is a renewable source of power.

patterns.pattern: [Event A] came [time]. ... [Event B] provided ...
patterns.explanation: 시간순으로 사건을 점점 빠르게 나열하여 가속을 느끼게 하는 내러티브 패턴.
examples: The telegraph came in the 1840s. Within decades, the telephone provided a new way to communicate.

drillQuestions.question: The printing press _____ this year in Kennedy's time scale.
The steam engine _____ a new source of power.

sentences.notes: 50년 비유의 핵심부. 인쇄기(약 570년 전)→증기기관(약 200년 전) 순으로 시간 간격이 급격히 줄어들어, 기술 발전의 가속을 체감하게 한다. "during this whole 50-year span"은 청중에게 비유를 상기시키는 삽입구.

koreanAlignment.en: The printing press came this year
less than 2 months ago
during this whole 50-year span of human history
the steam engine provided a new source of power

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 가속법(acceleration) — 시간 간격이 점점 짧아지며 발전 속도의 가속을 체감하게 하는 수사법

sentences.original: Newton explored the meaning of gravity. Last month electric lights and telephones and automobiles and airplanes became available.
sentences.translation: 뉴턴은 중력의 의미를 탐구했습니다. 지난달에 전등과 전화기와 자동차와 비행기가 등장했습니다.

grammarElements.text: electric lights and telephones and automobiles and airplanes
grammarElements.label: 주어 (4중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Scientists explore the mysteries of the universe.
Gravity keeps us grounded on Earth.
The product will be available next month.

expressions.usage: 비유적 시간 표현으로 약 100-150년 전에 해당

patterns.pattern: [Person] [verb] [X]. [Time expression] [A] and [B] and [C] and [D] became available.
patterns.explanation: 개별 발견 → 복수 발명의 폭발적 등장으로 가속을 표현하는 패턴.
examples: Einstein redefined physics. Within decades, nuclear energy and computers and satellites and the internet became available.

drillQuestions.question: Newton _____ the meaning of gravity.
Electric lights and telephones _____ available last month.

sentences.notes: "and"가 접속사로 세 번 반복되는 다중 접속법(polysyndeton). 보통 "A, B, C, and D"로 쓰지만 "A and B and C and D"로 쓰면 각 항목을 강조하며 목록이 길다는 인상을 준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Newton explored the meaning of gravity
Last month
electric lights and telephones and automobiles and airplanes
became available

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 다중 접속법(polysyndeton) — "and"를 반복하여 발명품의 쏟아지는 등장을 표현

sentences.original: Only last week did we develop penicillin and television and nuclear power, and now if America's new spacecraft succeeds in reaching Venus, we will have literally reached the stars before midnight tonight.
sentences.translation: 불과 지난주에야 우리는 페니실린과 텔레비전과 원자력을 개발했고, 이제 미국의 새 우주선이 금성에 도달하는 데 성공한다면, 우리는 문자 그대로 오늘 자정 전에 별에 도달하게 될 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: penicillin and television and nuclear power
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (3중 병렬)

grammarElements.text: if America's new spacecraft succeeds in reaching Venus
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: we will have literally reached the stars
grammarElements.label: 주절 (미래완료)

vocabulary.example: Penicillin was one of the most important medical discoveries.
The spacecraft launched successfully from Cape Canaveral.
We have literally changed the world.

expressions.usage: "Only + 부사구"가 문두에 오면 주어와 동사가 도치됨

expressions.phrase: reached the stars before midnight tonight

patterns.pattern: Only [time] did we [verb] ..., and now if [condition], we will have [past participle] ...
patterns.explanation: Only 부사 도치 구문으로 최근성을 강조하고, 미래완료로 비유를 완결하는 패턴.
examples: Only yesterday did we learn to fly, and now if we succeed, we will have colonized another planet.

drillQuestions.question: Only last week _____ we develop penicillin.
We will have literally _____ the stars before midnight.

sentences.notes: "Only last week did we develop"는 부정/제한 부사(Only)가 문두에 올 때 발생하는 도치(inversion). 이 50년 비유의 클라이맥스로, "오늘 자정 전"이라는 표현이 5만 년 역사의 마지막 순간을 나타내며 우주 탐사의 극적인 최신성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Only last week did we develop
penicillin and television and nuclear power
and now if America's new spacecraft succeeds in reaching Venus
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 이제 미국의 새 우주선이 금성에 도달하는 데 성공한다면

koreanAlignment.en: we will have literally reached the stars
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 문자 그대로 별에 도달하게 될 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: before midnight tonight

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 도치(inversion) + 시간 압축의 클라이맥스 — Only 도치로 최근성을 극대화하며, 50년 비유를 "오늘 자정"으로 마무리

sentences.original: This is a breathtaking pace, and such a pace cannot help but create new ills as it dispels old, new ignorance, new problems, new dangers.
sentences.translation: 이것은 숨 막히는 속도이며, 이러한 속도는 오래된 것을 몰아내면서 새로운 병폐, 새로운 무지, 새로운 문제, 새로운 위험을 만들 수밖에 없습니다.

grammarElements.text: new ignorance, new problems, new dangers
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (병렬 나열)

vocabulary.example: The view from the mountain was breathtaking.
We must address the ills of society.
Education can dispel ignorance.

patterns.pattern: [Subject] cannot help but [verb] ... as it [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: cannot help but (이중 부정 = 긍정) 구문으로 불가피성을 표현하고, as절로 동시 발생 상황을 묘사.
examples: Progress cannot help but create disruption as it displaces old systems.
Growth cannot help but bring challenges as it transforms communities.

drillQuestions.question: Such a pace cannot _____ but create new ills.
This is a _____ pace of progress.

sentences.notes: "cannot help but create"는 이중 부정으로 "만들 수밖에 없다"라는 강한 긍정. "as it dispels old"에서 old는 "오래된 것들(old ills)"의 줄임. new가 네 번 반복되어(new ills, new ignorance, new problems, new dangers) 새로운 문제의 다양성을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: This is a breathtaking pace
such a pace cannot help but create
new ills as it dispels old
new ignorance, new problems, new dangers
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 무지, 새로운 문제, 새로운 위험을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복법(anaphora) — "new"를 네 번 반복하여 진보가 수반하는 새로운 문제들을 각인

sentences.original: Surely the opening vistas of space promise high costs and hardships, as well as high reward.
sentences.translation: 분명 우주라는 열리는 전망은 높은 보상뿐만 아니라 높은 비용과 고난도 약속합니다.

vocabulary.example: The hilltop offered a magnificent vista of the valley.
Early settlers faced many hardships.
Hard work brings great rewards.

expressions.usage: 새로운 영역이나 가능성이 열릴 때의 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] promise(s) [A], as well as [B].
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 결과(reward)와 부정적 결과(costs)를 함께 제시하여 균형 있는 시각을 보여주는 패턴.
examples: New technologies promise great benefits, as well as new ethical challenges.
Exploration promises discovery, as well as danger.

drillQuestions.question: The opening _____ of space promise high costs and hardships.
High costs, as _____ as high reward.

sentences.notes: vista는 이탈리아어에서 온 차용어로 "경치, 전망"이라는 뜻. "opening vistas"는 "새롭게 열리는 전망"이라는 은유. promise가 "약속하다"가 아닌 "예고하다, 수반하다"의 뜻으로 사용.

koreanAlignment.en: Surely
the opening vistas of space
promise high costs and hardships
as well as high reward

sentences.original: So it is not surprising that some would have us stay where we are a little longer to rest, to wait.
sentences.translation: 그래서 일부 사람들이 우리가 조금 더 머물면서 쉬고 기다리기를 바라는 것은 놀랍지 않습니다.

grammarElements.text: that some would have us stay
grammarElements.label: that절 (진주어)

vocabulary.example: It is not surprising that people are worried.
They would have us believe it was safe.

expressions.usage: 반대 의견을 인정하면서 자신의 논지로 이끌 때

patterns.pattern: It is not surprising that some would have us [verb] ... to [verb], to [verb].
patterns.explanation: 반대 의견을 양보적으로 인정하고, 곧이어 반박하기 위한 설정 패턴.
examples: It is not surprising that critics would have us abandon the plan, to retreat, to give up.

drillQuestions.question: It is not _____ that some would have us stay.
Some would have us _____ where we are.

sentences.notes: "would have us stay"에서 have는 사역동사로 "우리가 머물도록 하다"의 뜻. would는 "바라다, 원하다"의 가정적 뉘앙스. 이 문장은 반대론자들의 입장을 소개하여, 다음 문장에서 반박하기 위한 수사적 장치.

koreanAlignment.en: it is not surprising that
some would have us stay
where we are a little longer
to rest, to wait

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보(concession) — 반대 의견을 먼저 인정한 뒤 반박하는 전형적 설득 기법

sentences.original: But this city of Houston, this State of Texas, this country of the United States was not built by those who waited and rested and wished to look behind them.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이 휴스턴이라는 도시, 이 텍사스라는 주, 이 미국이라는 나라는 기다리고 쉬며 뒤를 돌아보려 했던 사람들이 건설한 것이 아닙니다.

grammarElements.text: this country of the United States
grammarElements.label: 주어 (3 — 나라)

grammarElements.text: by those who waited and rested and wished to look behind them
grammarElements.label: 행위자 (by + 관계절)

vocabulary.example: This nation was built on principles of freedom.
Those who wished to stay behind were left.

expressions.phrase: was not built by those who
expressions.usage: 수동태 + 부정으로 건국 정신에 반하는 태도를 부정
과거에 집착하거나 퇴보하려는 태도를 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: This [A], this [B], this [C] was not built by those who [verb] and [verb] and [verb].
patterns.explanation: 3중 주어 병렬(도시→주→나라) + 3중 동사 병렬(부정적 행위)로 구성된 강력한 반박 패턴.
examples: This company, this industry, this economy was not built by those who hesitated and doubted and feared change.

drillQuestions.question: This country was not _____ by those who waited.
Those who waited and rested and wished to look _____ them.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 앞 문장의 "쉬고 기다리자"는 반대론에 대한 강력한 반박. this를 세 번 반복하며 규모를 확대(도시→주→나라)하고, 수동태 부정(was not built)으로 반대론의 정신이 미국의 건국 이념과 맞지 않음을 선언한다.

koreanAlignment.en: But this city of Houston
this State of Texas
this country of the United States
was not built by those who
waited and rested
and wished to look behind them

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 3중 확대법(amplification) + 반박(refutation) — 도시→주→나라로 규모를 확대하며 반대론을 건국 정신으로 일축

sentences.original: This country was conquered by those who moved forward--and so will space.
sentences.translation: 이 나라는 앞으로 나아간 사람들이 정복한 것입니다--우주도 마찬가지일 것입니다.

vocabulary.example: Ancient empires conquered vast territories.
We must move forward despite the challenges.

expressions.usage: "so + 조동사 + 주어" 도치 구문으로 동의/유사 상황을 간결하게 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] was [past participle] by those who [verb]--and so will [Y].
patterns.explanation: 과거의 성취를 근거로 미래의 도전을 선언하는 패턴. "and so will"은 "Y도 마찬가지로 될 것이다".
examples: This frontier was tamed by those who dared--and so will the next.
This revolution was led by those who believed--and so will the future.

drillQuestions.question: This country was _____ by those who moved forward.
And _____ will space be conquered.

sentences.notes: "and so will space"는 "and space will (be conquered) so (= likewise)"의 도치. 앞 문장에서 was not built로 부정한 뒤, 이 문장에서 was conquered로 긍정 선언을 하고, 마지막 네 단어 "and so will space"로 우주 탐사를 건국 정신의 연장으로 선언하는 극적 마무리.

koreanAlignment.en: This country was conquered
by those who moved forward
and so will space

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 유비(analogy) + 도치(inversion) — 서부 개척과 우주 탐사를 동일선상에 놓고, 짧은 도치 문장으로 결론을 극적으로 마무리

sentences.original: William Bradford, speaking in 1630 of the founding of the Plymouth Bay Colony, said that all great and honorable actions are accompanied with great difficulties, and both must be enterprised and overcome with answerable courage.
sentences.translation: 1630년 플리머스 베이 식민지 건설에 대해 말하면서 윌리엄 브래드포드는 모든 위대하고 명예로운 행동에는 큰 어려움이 따르며, 이 둘 모두를 그에 걸맞은 용기로 감행하고 극복해야 한다고 말했습니다.

grammarElements.text: speaking in 1630 of the founding of the Plymouth Bay Colony
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (현재분사)

grammarElements.text: that all great and honorable actions are accompanied with great difficulties
grammarElements.label: that절 (1)

grammarElements.text: and both must be enterprised and overcome with answerable courage
grammarElements.label: that절 (2)

vocabulary.example: It is an honorable cause.
Success is often accompanied with sacrifice.
(archaic) Great voyages must be enterprised with courage.
(archaic) We need answerable courage for such a task.

expressions.phrase: all great and honorable actions are accompanied with great difficulties
expressions.meaning: 모든 위대하고 명예로운 행동에는 큰 어려움이 따른다

patterns.pattern: [Historical figure], speaking in [year] of [topic], said that ...
patterns.explanation: 역사적 인물의 인용을 도입하는 격식 패턴. 삽입 분사구문으로 배경 설명.
examples: Lincoln, speaking in 1863 of the nation's purpose, said that government of the people shall not perish.

drillQuestions.question: All great actions are _____ with great difficulties.
Both must be enterprised and _____ with answerable courage.

sentences.notes: William Bradford는 메이플라워호를 타고 온 필그림 파더스의 지도자. 1630년 기록이므로 현대 영어와 다른 고어(archaic) 표현이 있다: "enterprised"(감행하다, 현대에는 거의 안 씀), "answerable courage"(그에 상응하는 용기). 케네디는 우주 탐사를 미국 건국의 정신적 연장선에 놓기 위해 이 인용을 사용.

koreanAlignment.en: William Bradford
speaking in 1630 of the founding of the Plymouth Bay Colony
koreanAlignment.ko: 1630년 플리머스 베이 식민지 건설에 대해 말하면서

koreanAlignment.en: said that all great and honorable actions
are accompanied with great difficulties
and both must be enterprised and overcome
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 둘 모두를 감행하고 극복해야 한다고

koreanAlignment.en: with answerable courage

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역사적 인용(historical quotation) — 건국시대 인물의 말을 인용하여 우주 탐사를 미국의 개척 전통과 연결

sentences.original: If this capsule history of our progress teaches us anything, it is that man, in his quest for knowledge and progress, is determined and cannot be deterred.
sentences.translation: 이 압축된 진보의 역사가 우리에게 무엇이든 가르쳐준다면, 그것은 인간이 지식과 진보를 향한 탐구에서 결연하며 저지될 수 없다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: If this capsule history of our progress teaches us anything
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: that man, in his quest for knowledge and progress, is determined and cannot be deterred
grammarElements.label: that절 (진주어)

vocabulary.example: Here is a capsule summary of the report.
The quest for knowledge drives human progress.
She was determined to succeed.
We will not be deterred by challenges.

expressions.phrase: if ... teaches us anything, it is that

patterns.pattern: If [X] teaches us anything, it is that [main message].
patterns.explanation: 결론을 도출하는 강력한 수사 패턴. 앞선 모든 논증을 하나의 교훈으로 요약.
examples: If history teaches us anything, it is that change is inevitable.
If this crisis teaches us anything, it is that we must be better prepared.

drillQuestions.question: If this capsule history _____ us anything, it is that man is determined.
Man, in his quest for knowledge, cannot be _____.

sentences.notes: "capsule history"는 앞서 전개한 50년 비유를 가리키는 자기 참조(self-reference). "determined and cannot be deterred"에서 deter(저지하다)와 determined(결연한)은 어원이 같은 단어로, 음운적 유사성이 수사적 효과를 높인다.

koreanAlignment.en: If this capsule history of our progress teaches us anything
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 압축된 진보의 역사가 우리에게 무엇이든 가르쳐준다면

koreanAlignment.en: it is that man
in his quest for knowledge and progress
is determined and cannot be deterred

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 교훈 도출(didactic conclusion) — "가르쳐준다면" 구문으로 50년 비유 전체의 교훈을 한 문장에 압축
